# Figure description: 4_Карта_прогнозов_M-42-III.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-III/4_Карта_прогнозов_M-42-III.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **forecast/predictive geological map** ("Карта прогнозов") with elements of mineral deposit distribution patterns — a specialized thematic map combining geological mapping with metallogenic/mineral potential assessment. It is a hand-drafted map at 1:200,000 scale, typical of Soviet-era regional geological surveys.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet:** М-42-III (indicated at top center)
- **Drawing number:** Чертёж №40
- **Year:** 1984
- **Scale:** 1:200,000
- **Organization:** Министерство геологии Казахской ССР, Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение
- **Compiler:** Central Geological Prospecting Expedition (Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция); compiled by **Науменко В.Б.**
- The map covers a portion of **northern Kazakhstan**, corresponding to the М-42-III sheet of the standard Soviet topographic/geological grid. The area encompasses a structurally complex zone with multiple geological units spanning Precambrian through Cenozoic age.

---

## Key Content

### Geological Units (Formation Symbols Visible):
The map displays a mosaic of geological units labeled with standard Soviet stratigraphic notation:

- **KZ** — Cenozoic (Кайнозой) cover — the most widespread unit, shown as blank/white areas distributed across the entire map, representing Quaternary–Neogene sedimentary cover
- **P₁Ar** — Lower Permian or Paleozoic-Archean basement rocks; appears in multiple locations, particularly in the western and southwestern portions
- **C₁tr, C₂tr, C₃tr** — Carboniferous units (Tournaisian, Visean, Serpukhovian stages), appearing in central and western portions
- **C₁sp, C₂sp** — Additional Carboniferous subdivisions
- **D₃** (with subscripts) — Upper Devonian units, appearing in the northwest corner and scattered locations
- **P₁Аm, P₂Аm** — Permian units (possibly Artinskian/Asselian); visible in central and southeastern portions
- **P₂sp, P₃sp** — Upper Permian units; southeast quadrant
- **P₁β** — Permian basaltic/volcanic rocks; south-central area
- **C₁Ca, C₂Ca, COCa** — Carboniferous units with calcium/carbonate designation; concentrated in the northern portion of the map

### Structural Features:
- **Red hatched zones (diagonal lines)** — These are the most visually dominant feature, appearing as broad diagonal bands crossing the map in two main orientations:
  - A **northwest-trending zone** crossing the northern third of the map
  - A **roughly east-west zone** crossing the middle third
  - A **northwest-trending zone** in the southwestern quadrant
  - These likely represent **predicted/forecast mineralization zones**, structural zones, or metallogenic belts — the core predictive element of this forecast map
- **Bold dash-dot lines labeled "Г"** — These mark major **fault or tectonic boundary lines** (Г likely = граница, boundary, or a named fault system), appearing as prominent curvilinear features separating major structural blocks. Three such lines are visible crossing different parts of the map.
- **Dashed contour lines with elevation values** (100, 200, 400, 600, 800, 1000, 1200 m) — topographic or depth-to-basement contours, particularly visible in the central-western portion
- **Dotted lines** — possibly geophysical survey lines or inferred geological contacts

### Mineral Occurrence Symbols:
- **Solid black dots (filled circles)** — concentrated in the northern portion of the map, particularly in the area labeled COCa/C₁Ca. These represent **known mineral occurrences or deposits** (probably copper given the "Cu" labels visible nearby)
- **"Cu" labels** — Copper mineralization indicators, scattered across the northern map area associated with Carboniferous carbonate units
- **"Co" labels** — Cobalt occurrences, also in the northern zone
- **"COCu", "C₁Cu"** — Combined unit/mineral labels indicating copper within specific Carboniferous units

### Color Scheme:
- The map is primarily **black and white** (pen-and-ink drafting)
- **Red** is used exclusively for the hatched forecast/mineralization zones — making them stand out as the primary predictive content
- **Green** — small patches of green color visible in the north-central area, possibly indicating a specific lithological unit (intrusive body or skarn)
